title: Chicken Chow Mein

overview.description: Tender chicken stir-fried with egg noodles, crisp vegetables and a savoury soy-based sauce. A quick Cantonese-inspired classic that's far fresher than a takeaway.

method: Cook the egg noodles according to the packet instructions until just tender. Drain thoroughly, rinse briefly under cold water, toss with a little vegetable oil and leave to drain well.
Heat a wok or large frying pan over a high heat until smoking hot.
Add the vegetable oil and stir-fry the chicken for 5–6 minutes until lightly browned. Remove and set aside.
Add the onion, carrots and pepper and stir-fry for about 3 minutes until just beginning to soften.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Return the chicken to the wok along with the cooked noodles.
Add the light soy sauce, oyster sauce, dark soy sauce, sesame oil and sugar.
Toss everything together over a high heat for 2–3 minutes until the noodles are well coated and beginning to brown slightly in places.
Stir through the beansprouts and most of the spring onions for the final minute of cooking.
Finish with the remaining spring onions and a little freshly ground black pepper before serving.

learning.did-you-know: The name 'chow mein' comes from Cantonese and literally means 'fried noodles'. The noodles—not the sauce—are the star of the dish.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to prepare noodles for stir-frying, how to cook over very high heat and how to avoid soggy stir-fries.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: One of the cornerstone Chinese recipes in the planner. It's quick, family-friendly and demonstrates authentic stir-frying techniques using simple ingredients.
chapman-tips: Drain the noodles thoroughly—dry noodles fry, wet noodles steam.
Have every ingredient prepared before turning on the heat.
Cook in batches if your pan is small to avoid overcrowding.
notes.batch-cooking: Best cooked fresh rather than frozen.
notes.leftovers: Reheat quickly in a hot wok or frying pan rather than the microwave if possible.
